key_insight: For intermetallic NRR catalysts, s- and p-band descriptors can be more informative than the traditional d-band center, especially for N2 and N2H adsorption.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, lower-cost screening of non-noble catalysts for sustainable ammonia synthesis using compact machine learning models guided by DFT data.
method: Built a high-throughput DFT dataset for 47 bimetallic intermetallic compounds across accessible surface sites.
Computed adsorption energies for N2, N2H, and NH3, generating about 1,200 labeled data points.
Trained ML models using intrinsic properties, band centers/fillings, and neighboring-atom Bader charges; interpreted them with SHAP.
evidence_signal: Models achieved mean absolute errors of 0.26 eV for N2, 0.39 eV for N2H, and 0.17 eV for NH3 using only 20 key features.
watch_item: The descriptor conclusions are based on a relatively small materials set, so broader transferability to new chemistries and surfaces should be validated.
paper_title: Going Beyond the d-band Center to Design Intermetallic Catalysts for Nitrogen Reduction: A High-Throughput DFT and Machine Learning Study
